stem: In a figure, lines m and n are parallel and are cut by transversal t. The angle formed between line m and transversal t measures 145°. The same-side interior angle b° lies between the two parallel lines on the same side of t. What is the value of b?
rationale:
Choice D is correct. When two parallel lines are cut by a transversal, same-side interior angles are supplementary. Therefore b + 145 = 180, so b = 35.

Choice A is incorrect because 145 would be the alternate-interior or corresponding angle, not the same-side interior angle. Choice B is incorrect because the transversal is not perpendicular to the parallel lines. Choice C is incorrect because 55 = 90 - 35 reflects an unjustified right-triangle assumption.
